statement: The shared D33 scanner is correct over a closed module-lexical source profile and FAILS CLOSED with the stable diagnostic 'unsupported-module-lexical-profile' on the exact out-of-profile set b1-b6; exactly one optional leading shebang (#! at byte 0, first line, LF/CRLF-terminated) is admitted and stripped before lexing.

The regex-vs-division decision is made by previous-significant-token: a `/` is DIVISION only after a value token (identifier, numeric/string/template/regex literal, this/super/true/false/null, )/]/value-}/postfix), and a REGEX after every other token including every reserved keyword. It is not a hand-maintained keyword allow-list.

The shared resolver and physicalContainment lstat every existing component of the ORIGINAL uncollapsed candidate path (original order) BEFORE any normalization that could collapse a `..` and erase a component; any symlink component is rejected; only genuine ENOENT is absence, every other lstat error fails closed. A backslash in a relative specifier is rejected before resolution.
The per-weaver REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS table is exactly the frozen normative table and is proven equal to the ledger's coverage validator by a unit.
LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS is deeply frozen and exactly {module, node:module} -> ["builtinModules", "isBuiltin"]; mutation attempts fail or leave it unchanged.
Each committed weaver implementation-file inventory is PROVEN equal to its derived accepted-relative-module-load closure (D33), never trusted. git -> {registry, git}; code -> {registry, code, util}.
